message:
feat: clarify CLI-strategy output when rate limits unavailable (#7)

The CLI fetch strategies (claude-cli-pty, codex-cli-rpc) succeed in
detecting the provider CLI and fetching identity, but can return
primary/secondary = None because the modern Claude (2.x) and Codex
CLIs don't expose rate-limit info via script-accessible subcommands
on Linux. The previous UX conflated "no data" with "success":

  INFO Fetch succeeded provider=claude strategy=claude-cli-pty
  "primary": null,
  "secondary": null,

…which looked like a parsing bug when it was really a CLI-capability
gap.

Two small clarifications:

**src/core/pipeline.rs** — `Fetch succeeded` log now distinguishes
between:
  - "Fetch succeeded (rate-limit data populated)" — actual quota data
    came back.
  - "Fetch completed (identity only — no rate-limit data available
    from this strategy)" — strategy ran cleanly but the CLI doesn't
    expose quota.

**src/render/human.rs** — When a provider block has identity/credits/
status but no rate-limit windows, we now prepend the line:
  "Rate limits: not available via this source (identity only)"
instead of rendering a block with a name/email but no visible rate
data. The truly-empty case still falls through to "No usage data
available" as before.

This is an honest messaging fix. A full rate-limit fetch on Linux
requires an OAuth-style auth path (Claude CLI doesn't have a
scriptable /limits equivalent at 2.1.x); that remains a larger
design item.
